Civic Kernel a thin protocol for public decisions
Know a case that belongs here? The corpus grows by a triage that shows its working: failures of process — a record quietly absent, altered, or ignored — are in scope; cases that would need the protocol to judge substance are refused, with the reason recorded. Bring the case you know to the open door, and the triage will answer in the open.
Every manifest and log entry shown here validates against the two waist schemas — that is the test. The stories are fiction anchored to fact: the people are invented, and every attack pattern cites a documented real-world precedent on its page. Verdicts are the harness's honest reading of the architecture document's own claims and limits, not endorsements. And the waist is not only for fiction: the prototype election validates against the same two schemas — check it in your browser.
